items.title: As five big U.S. banks report earnings on the same day, Citigroup is the one to watch
items.description: Among the largest U.S. banks, Citigroup is expected to show the greatest improvement by one important measure. But it still has a long way to go to reach its own performance target.

items.title: You don’t have to be rich to be financially independent. Here’s how to take control of your money.
items.description: Relying on a high salary or a hot stock is a dangerous trap. Why the most secure people plan for sudden crisis.

items.title: Earnings estimates have been following an unusual pattern this time around
items.description: Analysts generally lower their estimates in the months before earnings. But thanks to the energy and tech sectors, expectations have actually climbed heading into second-quarter results.

items.title: The No. 1 decision for aging retirees: Stay at home or move into a senior community?
items.description: Why staying in your longtime home requires more planning than you think.

items.title: The stock-market rally now hinges more on AI than oil
items.description: As earnings season kicks off, AI investment remains front and center.

items.title: My insurance company said my roof lost a few tiles. Loss adjusters found $10,000 in storm damage. How could this happen?
items.description: “My house shook violently from the wind.”

items.title: ‘I get $1,460 in Social Security’: My millionaire ex-husband, 74, refuses to pay alimony. What can I do?
items.description: “I have very little money, while my ex’s financial statements show assets in the millions.”

items.title: ‘It’s heartbreaking’: My brother claimed Social Security at 70. He died from cancer after one payment. Why wait to claim?
items.description: “I’ve always been a little skeptical of the government’s encouragement to delay claiming benefits.”

items.title: Is it better to spend my savings now so I can delay taking Social Security? How do I choose?
items.description: “By claiming earlier, I could preserve more of my portfolio and allow those assets to continue compounding.”

items.title: Your data built the AI boom — but Big Tech is pocketing 100% of the equity
items.description: Your share of the AI wealth is a right — not a handout. Here is how we claw back our money.
